name: Video Games
description: Classic and modern video games, developers, and characters

questions.q: In which game series does players guide the super-soldier Master Chief in fights against the Covenant?

source.notes: First released 2001 for Xbox. Developed by Bungie, then 343 Industries.

questions.q: Minecraft was originally created by a Swedish developer who goes by what online handle?

source.notes: Real name Markus Persson. Full game released 2011.

questions.q: In 2014, which tech giant acquired Minecraft developer Mojang?

source.notes: Purchased for $2.5 billion.

questions.q: Which company developed 'Street Fighter II,' a cornerstone of fighting games?

source.notes: Released 1991. 'Hadouken' and 'Shoryuken' became iconic moves.

questions.q: Which developer is behind games like 'World of Warcraft,' 'Overwatch,' and 'Diablo'?

source.notes: Founded 1991. Acquired by Microsoft via Activision Blizzard deal in 2023.

questions.q: Which publisher is responsible for the long-running 'Call of Duty' franchise?

source.notes: First released 2003. Annual releases since 2005.

questions.q: The mobile AR game 'Pokémon GO' was developed by which studio?

source.notes: Launched July 2016. Spun out from Google. Reached 1 billion downloads.

questions.q: Which social game app had players growing crops and harvesting animals on Facebook?

source.notes: Released by Zynga in 2009. Peaked at 83 million monthly users.

questions.q: In the 'Assassin's Creed' series, what is the name of the modern-day corporation secretly a front for the Templars?

source.notes: Uses the Animus to access genetic memories.

questions.q: Grand Theft Auto V, one of the best-selling games of all time, was released in which year?

source.notes: September 17, 2013. Has sold over 200 million copies.

questions.q: Which accessory was required to play Duck Hunt on the original Nintendo Entertainment System?

source.notes: Light gun accessory. Released with the NES in 1985.

questions.q: The 'Red Dead Redemption' games are set in what time period?

source.notes: Developed by Rockstar. First released 2010; 'RDR2' in 2018.

questions.q: Which game, released in 1991 by Psygnosis, had players guide thousands of small rodents to safety?

source.notes: Created by DMA Design (later renamed Rockstar North).

questions.q: Sid Meier's 'Civilization,' the iconic turn-based strategy game, was first released in which year?

source.notes: Published by MicroProse. Series continues with 'Civilization VII' in 2025.

questions.q: The protagonist of The Legend of Zelda series is named what?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: fixed garbled original_a metadata (was 'The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom'). Zelda is the princess Link rescues. Created by Shigeru Miyamoto.

questions.q: Which fast blue mascot is the face of Sega video games?

source.notes: First game released 1991. Rival mascot to Nintendo's Mario.

questions.q: Who designed the original Super Mario Bros. and The Legend of Zelda?

source.notes: Joined Nintendo in 1977. Also created Donkey Kong, Star Fox, and Pikmin.

questions.q: Tetris was invented by which programmer in 1984?

source.notes: Soviet programmer. Game Boy bundled version (1989) made it a global hit.

questions.q: Which company developed the original 'Pac-Man' arcade game in 1980?

source.notes: Designed by Toru Iwatani. Name comes from Japanese onomatopoeia 'paku-paku' (chomping).

questions.q: Which studio developed the 'Grand Theft Auto' and 'Red Dead Redemption' franchises?

source.notes: Parent company Take-Two Interactive. Founded 1998 by Sam and Dan Houser.

questions.q: In the Mario Bros. series, what is the name of Mario's younger, taller, green-clad brother?

source.original_a: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
source.notes: First appeared in Mario Bros. (1983). Star of Luigi's Mansion series.

questions.q: The original 'Pong,' often cited as the first commercially successful video game, was released in what year?

source.notes: Released by Atari. Designed by Allan Alcorn as a training exercise.
